title: astragalus gummifer
intro: Astragalus gummifer, also known as tragacanth, is a type of perennial plant that belongs to the legume family. It is native to the Middle East and Central Asia, and its roots and sap contain a thick, gummy substance called gum tragacanth, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Astragalus gummifer include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and provide antioxidant effects. The plant has been used in various herbal preparations, such as capsules, teas, and topical creams, to promote health benefits such as improved gut health, reduced joint pain, and enhanced immune function.
